claim: WHO estimates 5.4 million snakebites, 138000 deaths, 400000 amputations annually, with SSA and South Asia carrying 85 percent, and launched a strategy targeting 50 percent mortality reduction by 2030.
method: Reviewed WHO strategy document, burden estimates, and antivenom access analysis.
limitations: Snakebite incidence is underreported due to reliance on health-facility data and prevalent traditional-healer care-seeking.
Burden estimates are modeled from surveys in limited countries.
